The frontend (gormaran.io) is serving HTML. The backend at gormaran-growth-hub.onrender.com returns a 404 for the root path but that's normal for an API server.

Since the HTTP responses are fine (200), the issue might be something specific in the app. Let me think about what could be broken...

The last commit was "Fix Instagram Carousel Generator location to Content Creation" - this is related to UI changes.


I should look at the git history to see what actually changed in that commit and whether something in the JavaScript or component logic got broken.

         buildUserMessage: (inputs) =>
           `Create a complete logo design system:\n\n**Brand Name:** ${inputs.brand_name}\n**Industry:** ${inputs.industry}\n**Logo Style:** ${inputs.style}\n**Preferred Colors:** ${inputs.colors || 'Open to suggestions'}\n**Brand Values / Personality:** ${inputs.values || 'Not specified'}\n**Avoid:** ${inputs.avoid || 'Nothing specific'}\n\nGenerate 3 distinct logo concepts with full color palettes, typography stacks, design guidelines and AI image prompts.`,
       },
+      {
+        id: 'instagram-carousel',
+        name: 'Instagram Carousel Generator',
+        description: 'Generate slide-by-slide carousel content with hooks, body copy and CTAs — upload a reference image for style matching',
+        icon: '🎠',
+        hasRefImage: true,
+        inputs: [
+          { id: 'topic', label: 'Carousel Topic / Hook', type: 'textarea', placeholder: 'e.g., 5 reasons why your Instagram reach is dying (and how to fix it)', required: true },
+          { id: 'slides', label: 'Number of Slides', type: 'select', options: ['3 slides', '5 slides', '7 slides', '10 slides'], required: true },
+          { id: 'niche', label: 'Niche / Industry', type: 'text', placeholder: 'e.g., personal finance, fitness, marketing, real estate', required: true },
+          { id: 'audience', label: 'Target Audience', type: 'text', placeholder: 'e.g., female entrepreneurs aged 25–35', required: true },
+          { id: 'tone', label: 'Tone', type: 'select', options: ['Educational', 'Inspirational', 'Conversational', 'Bold & Direct', 'Storytelling', 'Humorous'], required: true },
+          { id: 'cta', label: 'Call to Action (last slide)', type: 'text', placeholder: 'e.g., Save this post, Follow for more, DM me "CAROUSEL"', required: true },
+          { id: 'extra', label: 'Extra Context (optional)', type: 'textarea', placeholder: 'e.g., brand colors, key message, product to promote, specific stats to include' },
+        ],
+        systemPrompt: `You are an expert Instagram content strategist who creates viral carousel posts. Every carousel must stop the scroll, deliver real value, and drive saves and shares.
+
+Output each slide clearly labelled. For every slide include:
+- **Slide headline** (max 8 words — bold, punchy, curiosity-driven)
+- **Body copy** (2–4 short lines max — easy to read on mobile)
+- **Visual note** (1 line describing what to show: graphic, photo, icon, color background, etc.)
+
+Structure:
+**SLIDE 1 — COVER (Hook)**
+Make it impossible to scroll past. Use a bold claim, surprising stat, or direct question. Include the topic keyword.
+
+**SLIDE 2 to N-1 — VALUE SLIDES**
+Each slide = one clear point. Use numbered lists where possible. Short sentences. No fluff.
+
+**LAST SLIDE — CTA**
+Reinforce the main takeaway. Add the CTA naturally. Invite to save, share, or reply.
+
+---
+After all slides, add:
+
+**CAPTION**
+- Hook line (first 125 characters must hook — no emojis at the start)
+- 3–5 sentence body expanding on the carousel topic
+- CTA matching the last slide
+- 5 niche hashtags + 5 broad hashtags
+
+**COVER DESIGN TIPS**
+- Font suggestion
+- Background color / style recommendation
+- Layout tip
+
+Be specific to the niche and audience provided. No generic advice.`,
+        buildUserMessage: (inputs) =>
+          `Create a complete Instagram carousel:\n\n**Topic:** ${inputs.topic}\n**Slides:** ${inputs.slides}\n**Niche:** ${inputs.niche}\n**Target Audience:** ${inputs.audience}\n**Tone:** ${inputs.tone}\n**CTA:** ${inputs.cta}${inputs.extra ? `\n**Extra Context:** ${inputs.extra}` : ''}${inputs._ref_image_b64 ? '\n\n[Reference image provided — match the visual style, color palette and branding in the design tips.]' : ''}\n\nGenerate every slide fully. Include caption and design tips at the end.`,
+        maxTokens: 6000,
+      },
     ],
   },
   {
@@ -880,57 +931,6 @@ Write complete, conversion-optimized landing page copy:
         buildUserMessage: (inputs) =>
           `Write high-converting landing page copy:\n\n**Product/Service:** ${inputs.product}\n**Primary Offer/CTA:** ${inputs.offer}\n**Target Visitor:** ${inputs.audience || 'General visitors'}\n**Primary Benefit:** ${inputs.main_benefit || 'Not specified'}\n**Pain Points:** ${inputs.pain_points || 'Not specified'}\n**Social Proof:** ${inputs.social_proof || 'None available yet'}\n\nCreate the complete landing page copy with all sections.`,
       },

content.thinking:
The last commit only moved the Instagram Carousel Generator from one category to another in `categories.js`. This doesn't seem like it could break the entire site.

Let me check if there might be something else. The frontend is serving HTML and the backend is running. Let me look at the actual browser console errors by checking the JS bundle more carefully, or look at what might be causing the issue.

Wait, the user said "no funciona la web ni en el ordenador ni en el móvil" - maybe it's a runtime error, not a server/deployment issue. Let me check if there's maybe a syntax error in categories.js, or if the build on the server might be stale (if Render is serving an old build).


The site is returning a 200 status with HTML content, so the server's responding fine, but there's likely a JavaScript error preventing the app from initializing. I should build the project locally to catch any compilation errors that might not be obvious from just reviewing the code.

content.text: El cambio fue solo mover el Instagram Carousel de categoría. Vamos a buscar errores de sintaxis o de runtime.

content.thinking:
The build completes with just warnings from react-snap (which uses puppeteer to pre-render). The errors are from a third-party script (contentsquare) and are not related to our code. The build seems fine.

So the build works. The server responds. But the user says it doesn't work. Let me think about what "doesn't work" means...

Maybe the issue is with the production deployment on Render. The site at gormaran.io is 200, but maybe the Render service for the frontend hasn't been deployed with the latest code. Or maybe there's some other issue.
 
I'm noticing the Cloudflare and Render headers in the response, so the traffic is definitely routing through both services. The real question is whether the Render deployment is stale or if there's a configuration issue preventing the latest build from being served. Let me check if there are any JavaScript errors on the page itself or if the deployment hasn't picked up the recent changes.

content.thinking:
I see the issue! The production site at gormaran.io is serving `main.9ec1f9fe.js` but the local build just generated `main.98102fca.js`. This means the site on Render hasn't been updated with the latest code. But wait, these are different hashes - the production one is older. 

BUT - if the site is serving main.9ec1f9fe.js and it loads fine (200 OK), why would the site not work?

Wait, the user asked me what's wrong - "no funciona la web ni en el ordenador ni en el móvil". 

So the frontend loads successfully, but maybe there's a runtime error causing the white screen or broken functionality. The production site is running an older version of the code, which could mean the backend API calls are failing or the backend itself has issues. Let me check what the backend health check revealed.

The Stripe integration being disabled might be critical for the app to work, or this could stem from that security-focused commit that removed the n8n chat feature. I need to understand what "doesn't work" actually means from the user's perspective—is it a blank page, broken tools, or a login issue? The key insight is that the production deployment is serving an older JavaScript bundle than what's in the local build, so the latest changes haven't made it to production yet.

content.text:
Hay un desfase: la web en producción sirve `main.9ec1f9fe.js` pero el build local generó `main.98102fca.js`. Las últimas actualizaciones no están desplegadas en Render.

¿Puedes ser más específico sobre qué es lo que falla? Por ejemplo:
- ¿Pantalla en blanco?
- ¿Carga pero las herramientas de IA no responden?
- ¿El login no funciona?
- ¿Hay algún mensaje de error?
